mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment trigger fires, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth consistently outpacing headline inflation indicates healthy demand-pull expansion without overheating, supporting corporate profit growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The positive spread between quarterly GDP growth and annualized CPI inflation coincides with negative S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 110 index level signals supply-side bottlenecks or collapsing aggregate demand, reducing industrial sector earnings and dragging down the broader equity index over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral during sustained periods where industrial production trades below the 110 threshold, yielding a negative sign accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent triggers a negative consumer wealth effect and reduces construction-related corporate revenues, depressing near-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not decline in the subsequent week when housing starts fall more than 10 percent year-over-year, or the signal generates a positive predictive sign accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand and sustained consumer spending power, driving positive corporate revenue growth and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between monthly payroll changes and 60-day forward index returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated federal funds rates and persistently high inflation compresses real yields and equity risk premiums, forcing multiple contraction and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Periods where both the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent and CPI remains above 3.0 percent do not predict negative S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer revolving credit balances indicate expanding household leverage and rising confidence, which fuels near-term retail sales and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly higher when revolving credit balances cross the 400 billion threshold, or the signal produces a negative sign accuracy below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward window.
